task_completion.description: Verify healthcheck configuration is added to the product-catalog service using curl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing docker-compose.yml with a 'healthcheck:' block under 'product-catalog'. The 'test:' command must use 'curl' targeting port 3000 and the '/healthz' endpoint.

task_completion.description: Verify healthcheck configuration is added to the order-service using curl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing docker-compose.yml with a 'healthcheck:' block under 'order-service'. The 'test:' command must use 'curl' targeting port 5000 and the '/health' endpoint.

task_completion.description: Verify healthcheck configuration is added to the db service using pg_isready.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing docker-compose.yml with a 'healthcheck:' block under 'db'. The 'test:' command must use 'pg_isready'.

task_completion.description: Verify healthcheck configuration is added to the cache service using redis-cli.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing docker-compose.yml with a 'healthcheck:' block under 'cache'. The 'test:' command must use 'redis-cli' (e.g., 'redis-cli ping').

task_completion.description: Verify restart policies are added to all four services.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing a 'restart:' directive (e.g., 'always', 'unless-stopped', or 'on-failure') under all four services (product-catalog, order-service, db, cache) in docker-compose.yml.

task_completion.description: Verify the healthy status of all services is documented in ~/Desktop/health_status.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'cat ~/Desktop/health_status.txt' or a text editor open with this file, displaying evidence that the services are healthy (e.g., pasted output from 'docker ps' or 'docker compose ps' showing the '(healthy)' state).

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually started the containers to check their health status.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal showing commands like 'docker compose up' and 'docker ps' or 'docker compose ps' being executed to observe the actual '(healthy)' status.

integrity.description: Verify the docker-compose.yml file was actually saved with the modifications.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the file being saved and exited in an editor (like nano/vim) or a subsequent 'cat docker-compose.yml' confirming the changes persist on disk.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just write 'all services healthy' into the text file without actually configuring and testing the healthchecks.
integrity.visual_evidence: The creation and writing of ~/Desktop/health_status.txt should occur after modifying docker-compose.yml and running the containers, ideally containing actual command output rather than a manually typed guess.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project directory is /home/ga/projects/healthcheck-lab. Services to configure in docker-compose.yml are 'product-catalog', 'order-service', 'db', and 'cache'. Container names are 'healthlab-catalog', 'healthlab-orders', 'healthlab-db', and 'healthlab-cache'. The evidence file must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/health_status.txt. Scoring requires 80 points for healthchecks (20 per service), 10 for restart policies, and 10 for the evidence file.
